text.title: Confidence Calibration for Patterns
text.summary: Confidence scoring levels, update formula, decay rules, and common calibration errors.

list.item: 0.9+: High — 5+ uses, validated across contexts, no recent failures
0.3-0.49: Experimental — newly identified, not yet validated

section.title: Update Formula

rule.statement: Apply the confidence update formula after each task outcome: success → min(0.95, score + 0.05); partial → score * 0.95; failure → max(0.30, score - 0.15).
rule.rationale: Symmetric updates would allow a single failure to destroy high-confidence patterns. The asymmetric formula requires consistent failures to reduce confidence significantly.

section.reference: Full confidence updater script that patches patterns.md in place

antipattern.description: Agents assign initial confidence of 0.9 to new patterns because the first use succeeded.
antipattern.reason: One success does not validate cross-context applicability. New patterns start at 0.5 regardless of first-use outcome.

antipattern.description: Confidence scores over 90 days old treated as current without verification.
antipattern.reason: Framework upgrades, architecture changes, and library version bumps can invalidate high-confidence patterns silently. Treat scores older than 90 days as unreliable until re-validated.

antipattern.description: Multiple agents writing to patterns.md concurrently.
antipattern.reason: Concurrent writes produce last-write-wins corruption of confidence scores. In multi-agent workflows, serialize memory writes through a single coordinator agent.
